summary.intent: Fix compilation errors on the `fix-mcp-naming` branch caused by missing `display_name` field in `McpServerEntry` struct initializers in `doctor_commands.rs`.
summary.outcome: Added `display_name: None` to all 5 `McpServerEntry` struct initializers in `doctor_commands.rs`, verified clean compilation, committed, and pushed to the remote branch.

repo: The `moltis_config::schema::McpServerEntry` struct has a `display_name` field (likely recently added as part of the MCP naming work) that must be explicitly set â€” it has no default and is not `#[derive(Default)]`.
CI runs a lint/build step (`local/lint`) that caught the missing field; it took 376s to surface the error.

code.finding: McpServerEntry initializer missing `display_name` field â€” fixed with `display_name: None`

workflow: The branch was pushed before verifying compilation; the CI lint step caught the struct field errors post-push.
Worktrees are used under `~/.superset/worktrees/moltis/temikus/` for branch isolation.

friction: The `display_name` field was missing from all 5 `McpServerEntry` initializers in `doctor_commands.rs` â€” this was likely introduced when the field was added to the schema as part of the broader MCP naming refactor but `doctor_commands.rs` was not updated to match.
Errors were only caught after pushing, requiring an additional fix commit rather than catching locally before push.
open_items: Verify whether other crates or files that construct `McpServerEntry` also need `display_name` populated with a real value rather than `None` â€” the doctor commands may need to surface display names in their output eventually.
Consider whether `display_name` should be `#[serde(default)]` or have a `Default` impl to prevent similar breakage when the schema evolves.
